steps.title: Identify the structure around the blank
steps.content: Before the blank the adjective “poetic” needs a noun to modify; after the blank the text begins a noun phrase (“compressed lines…”) that is the first of three items separated by semicolons.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule and eliminate the others
steps.content: A colon introduces a list or elaboration, so the correct punctuation after the noun “style” is a colon, followed by the adjective “terse,” to describe the first list item. The other three choices break the rule: A uses a period, creating a sentence fragment; B inserts a comma that fails to link the noun to the list and creates a confusing modifier; D uses a semicolon, which only joins two independent clauses, not a noun to its list. That matches choice C — C is correct.
